external.description:
このX投稿の補足と解説（2026年6月26日投稿）

投稿内容（原文）
めっちゃ最良な学習習慣ですね。  
これは教員志望でも求められるもので、さらにテスト1週間前に復習とか自分で調べて確認して考えたのを他者に相談・確認とかもやれたら尚良い。

引用元投稿の概要
引用されているのは、塾講師のアカウント（@TYPFNJvx94LvMko）の投稿です。

内容の要点：
理想的な中学受験の学習姿勢として、「自分でテスト範囲を確認して、自分で考えて勉強する」自立した習慣を高く評価。
親が小4からすべて管理している子に負けて落ちたとしても、「中学以降で逆転する」と述べています。

解説・補足

このやり取りは、子どもの「自立した学習習慣」の重要性をテーマにした教育論です。

1. 投稿者の主張のポイント
自立学習の価値を強く肯定している。
特に「自分で範囲を確認→自分で考える→勉強する」というプロセスを「最良」と評価。
これを教員志望者にも求められる資質として位置づけている（教師になる上でも、主体的に学ぶ姿勢が重要という意味）。

2. さらに「尚良い」とした追加要素
投稿者が補足で挙げているのは、より高度な学習スキル：
テスト1週間前の自主復習
自分で調べて確認する
考えたことを他者（先生・親・友人）に相談・確認する

これらはまさにアクティブラーニングやメタ認知（自分の学習を自分で管理する力）の要素で、現代の教育現場（特に大学入試や教員採用試験）でも重視される能力です。

3. 背景となる教育観
親の管理 vs 子どもの自立という、よくある中学受験あるあるの対立軸に対する見解。
短期的な「合格」より、長期的な学習者としての成長を優先する考え方。
日本の教育では「詰め込み」「親の力任せ」の成功例も多いが、それとは対極の「自走型学習」を理想としている。

総評
この投稿は、「今は負けてもいいから、自立した学習習慣を身につけさせよう」という、受験競争の中で忘れがちな本質を思い出させる良質な意見です。特に教員を目指す人や子育て中の親にとって、共感しやすい内容だと思います。

中学受験を「通過点」として捉え、中学以降の自立した学びを重視する視点は、とても健全ですね。

external.description:
このポストの解説・補足

このXポストは、歴史オタクあるあるを突いた引用ポストに対する返信です。

引用されているポストの内容
『細川？　大内？　三好？　そいつらって天下取ってなくない？　じゃあカスでしょ』  
歴オタ１「は？」  
歴オタ２「今なんと？」  
（以下略）

これは、戦国時代に有力だったが最終的に天下を取れなかった武将・大名（細川氏、大内氏、三好氏など）を、「天下を取っていないから価値がない」と一刀両断する素人発言を皮肉ったものです。歴オタから見ると非常に的外れで、腹立たしい典型例として描かれています。

本ポストの返信（ウラディスラオ氏）
戦後の総理の一人に細川家当主の細川護熙が居ます。  
背後に小沢一郎が居たんだけどね。

ポイント解説
細川護熙（ほそかわ もりひろ）：  
  細川氏は室町時代から続く名門（足利将軍家の支流・三管領の一角）。戦国時代には細川京兆家として畿内を牛耳るなど絶大な権力を握っていました（特に細川晴元・高国時代）。  
  その末裔である細川護熙は、1993〜1994年に内閣総理大臣を務めました（日本新党→新進党政権の初代首相）。つまり「戦国時代の有力家系が、現代の日本で実際に総理大臣を出した」という事実を指摘しています。

「天下を取ってなくない？」という論理の愚かさを間接的に突いている  
  戦国時代に「天下を取れなかった」からといって家系そのものの歴史的意義や影響力を否定するのはナンセンスだ、というメッセージです。細川家は実際に現代の首相を出しているのですから。

「背後に小沢一郎」  
  1993年の非自民連立政権（細川政権）誕生の立役者は小沢一郎（当時自民党を離党して新生党を結成）でした。細川護熙は象徴的な首相で、実質的な政権運営・駆け引きの中心には小沢がいた、という当時の政治状況を補足しています。

まとめ（このポストが言いたいこと）
戦国史で「天下を取ったか否か」だけで人物・家柄を評価するのは浅はか。
細川家のような名門は、数百年にわたって日本史に影響を与え続け、現代政治にもつながっている。
「天下取れなかった＝カス」という極端な二元論を笑っている。

歴オタがイラッとする典型的な「結果至上主義的历史観」に対するカウンターとして、なかなか上手い返しだと思います。

Der Anime entsteht bei Acca effe und Frontier Engine unter der Regie von Kazuya Fujishiro (Dropkick on My Devil!). Die Serienkomposition übernimmt Yasushi Hirano (City Hunter, Dragon Ball), während das Drehbuch von Kayō Shimoyama und Makuto Okimiru stammt. Das Charakterdesign stammt von Megumi Ishihara (Fate/stay night, Fortune Arterial: Akai Yakusoku). Die Soundregie liegt bei Tadashi Jinbo (D4DJ Petit Mix, Umayuru), die Soundproduktion übernimmt Bisei und ISAO komponiert die Musik, während Frontier Works für die Musikproduktion verantwortlich ist.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Yuusuke arbeitet am liebsten allein, hält Abstand zu anderen Menschen und hat nach der plötzlichen Pleite seiner Firma endgültig jeden Antrieb verloren. Tagelang verkriecht er sich in seiner Wohnung, bis ihn bei einem seltenen Gang nach draußen ein unbekannter Mann angreift und in den Arm beißt. Nach mehreren Tagen Fieber erwacht er in einer Welt, die nicht mehr dieselbe ist: Überall lauern Zombies, die letzten Menschen verstecken sich vor dem Untergang. Doch ausgerechnet ihn scheinen die Untoten nicht als Beute zu erkennen.

»Yūsuke Takemura« von Wataru Katō
»Misuki Fujino« von Yuno Azuma

external.description: India's technology services industry is expected to remain a key partner in global enterprise transformation as artificial intelligence (AI) adoption accelerates, with the focus shifting from routine IT services to AI-led business transformation, according to Nasscom. The industry body said AI is changing the way technology services are delivered rather than reducing their relevance. It expects demand to rise for enterprise modernisation, AI governance, cybersecurity, data readiness and intelligent operations as organisations move AI projects from pilot stages to large-scale deployment. The assessment was shared at the Nasscom US CEO Forum held in New York, where industry leaders and government representatives discussed the future of India's technology services sector in the AI era. According to Nasscom, nearly 25 per cent of technology services companies have already deployed AI use cases into production. The sector is generating an estimated USD 10 billion to USD 12 billion in AI services revenue, while more than two million professionals have AI skills and around 85 per cent of technology service providers have developed agentic AI platforms. Putin's Chilling 'Doomsday Radio' Suddenly Broadcasts 20 Secret Codes as Russia 'Plots False Flag Attack'
->International Business Times | More on "Russia doomsday radio secret codes" at BigEarthData.ai | #RussiaUkraineWar #PutinWarCriminal

external.description: Russia's mysterious 'Doomsday Radio' has thrust itself back into the spotlight after broadcasting 20 cryptic messages in a single day, an unusually intense burst of transmissions that came as a senior European official warned the Kremlin could be preparing a 'false flag' attack. The eerie activity from Putin's Doomsday Radio, officially known as UVB-76, immediately fuelled fresh speculation online. Among the strange coded words reportedly transmitted were 'AMILLOKUS', 'VOLOKITA' and 'FLUSOBOB', while others loosely translated as 'spring', 'support' and 'rubbing'. record.text: Fox News struggles to 'carry water for Trump' with awkward America 250 coverage

external.description:
The Great American State Fair kicked off on Wednesday night, June 24 with a speech by President Donald Trump on the National Mall in Washington, DC. The Trump administration is touting the Fair, which continues through July 10, as a celebration of the United States' 250th anniversary. But Trump's critics are arguing that the opening felt more like a partisan MAGA rally than an actual celebration of America's achievements as a democratic republic.

 One of those critics is Media Matters' Matt Gertz. During a late June appearance on The New Republic's podcast "The Daily Blast," Gertz stressed that turnout at the Great American State Fair's opening was a major disappointment — citing the MAGA themes as a key factor and attacking Fox News' glowing coverage as painfully awkward.

Fox News, according to Gertz, went out of its way to "carry water" for Trump with its Fair coverage.

Gertz told podcast host Sargent, "It's been a tough few months for people who have to carry water for Donald Trump every night…. And basically, they're trying to use what should be a celebration of the Declaration of Independence, of America’s 250th birthday, as a partisan wedge issue, as a cudgel against the Democratic Party, while simultaneously talking up Donald Trump and his ability to pull a huge crowd and get them together for a big rally. So, the failure, I think, of the kickoff event is a pretty big problem for them in the medium term as they try to keep that message going over the next 10, 12 days."

Gertz described attendance on Wednesday night as a major disappointment.

The Media Matters report told Sargent, a former Washington Post columnist, "Originally, this was supposed to be a big concert with a bunch of different artists who were scheduled to play. But as it became more and more clear that these Freedom 250 events are extremely partisan, the artists decided to drop out. And eventually Trump kind of threw up his hands and said, instead of having this concert, we’re going to launch the state fair with what he called the greatest rally ever. It doesn’t seem to have worked out that way."

Sargent pointed out that Trump "seems very sensitive" about the "low turnout" on June 24, lamenting that he "tried to turn a celebration of America's 250th birthday into a Trump rally." When the "Daily Blast" host described Trump's Great American State Fair speech as an "imperial, dictatorial display of self-glorification," he got no argument from Gertz.

The Media Matters reporter told Sargent, "I mean, I think what we have here is a president who does not respect any sort of separation between himself and the country at large. And so, he views the idea of celebrating the nation's birthday as one and the same with celebrating himself. I think there's no clearer way to see that than how he decided to kick off the festivities with what he personally described as a rally speech — a partisan speech in which he sort of ran down what he claims are his accomplishments and talked about himself, rather than the nation, rather than what brings us together. And that becomes more and more fraught as he becomes more and more unpopular."
external.title: Fox News struggles to 'carry water for Trump' with awkward America 250 coverage

record.text:
It's also very frustrating that there are so few leaders and civs in the base game. They are nearly doing Sims-level of profit extraction, practically. 

All-in-all though there's so much strategic depth here I think I'll be saying "just one more turn" for a while to come.

As far as the flaws go, it's annoying that you can't raze cities without a penalty because the AI are really bad about forward settling you in stupid ways, and the same is true that relationships are so easily marred by *their* stupid action.

The distinction between towns and cities is also pretty nice in that it lets you play a mix of tall & wide, and "specialists" are an interesting layer on that too. I'm not thrilled with overbuilding as it is but it's also an interesting strategic wrinkle to play with.

ps: não uso neoludismo ou ludismo de forma pejorativa. Apenas como distinção de posições, porque para os colegas neoluditas, o tech ceticismo é mais ameno nas posições. Nem todo mundo que se diferencia do ludismo nega seu valor histórico ou faz caricatura do movimento, relaxem.

Não usei pejorativo. Apenas eu não sou ludita ou neoludita. Me considero adepta ao tecno ceticismo, me diferenciando dos amigos meus neoluditas. Foi apenas uma distinção, mas entendo que há quem use de forma pejorativa. Não foi meu caso e eu conheço bem a história do ludismo.

E entenda os impactos socioecológicos, porque eles são agregados. Enquanto não resolvermos a contradição humano x capital x natureza, é feio usar visão de túnel de "nem toda IA" - afinal, toda IA hoje faz parte do sistema de digitalização generalizada. O filtro de "IA de esquerda" é imaginário.

If you are trying to get ChatGPT to understand the formatting on a Microsoft Word file (.docx), e.g. strikethrough or highlighting, it won't be able to see it on upload.  However, compress the file (.zip) and then ChatGPT _can_ see your lovely formatting. Yay!
